originalBehavior: The published article loads through a Nuxt shell with a large vendored chunk graph, route-local fonts, many interactive mounts, and a longform markdown body rendered into one page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, Nuxt runtime, and chunk graph while rewriting only the subpath-hosting metadata, asset paths, and shared-footer provenance hook.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream RSS, Twitter, e-mail, or Fathom provenance surfaces.
The route keeps the original article structure instead of condensing the collision walkthrough into a custom rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the flattened route root, full local chunk graph, and local font handoff as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the Nuxt runtime loads locally, the article mounts on the local root, and the route stays runtime-clean without upstream asset dependencies.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a large collision canvas and early time or velocity controls that introduce the basic scene and frame-by-frame collision intuition.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening collision canvas and early control surfaces under the original published runtime.
notes: The opening canvas remains mounted on first load at the local route root.
The route still exposes the published button and range-control surfaces instead of replacing them with static screenshots.
evidence: The route-specific smoke contract includes the opening canvas, local essay fonts, and early control presence as explicit route checks.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the article boots on the flattened root without the vendored not-found card surfacing in the public body.

originalBehavior: The published article continues with vector, normal, and dot-product sections that combine canvas scenes, inline formulas, and later control paths across the longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later interactive sections, inline formulas, and article-long runtime mounted under the original vendored assets.
notes: The article’s later interactive and formula sections remain in the same order and typography as the published page.
The local route keeps the custom essay fonts local so the longform styling and section headings remain intact under subpath hosting.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the later collision-normal and dot-product article flow as parity-sensitive route behavior.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route stays responsive, keeps local script and font handoff intact, and remains free of upstream runtime requests.
